title: M1 scenario suite
description:
End-to-end scenarios that validate the M1 promise.

**Deliverables:**
- tests/scenarios/m1-happy-path.scenario — init → epic → tasks with deps → ACs
  added → tasks claimed and closed in dependency order → board reflects state
- tests/scenarios/m1-ac-enforcement.scenario — close fails with incomplete ACs,
  succeeds after ACs checked, force-close with reason works
- tests/scenarios/m1-conflict-resolution.scenario — two branches add tasks under
  the same epic; merge via JSON merge driver succeeds; both tasks present on main
- tests/scenarios/m1-index-rebuild.scenario — delete index.db; next command
  rebuilds cleanly; queries return same results as before deletion
- tests/scenarios/m1-clean-clone.scenario — clone the repo; first firetrail
  command works without manual setup

**Acceptance:**
- All M1 scenarios pass
- Total scenario suite runs in under 30 seconds (per ROADMAP §M1 success criteria)
- Each scenario has clear failure messages when assertions fail
- Verifier subagent has independently confirmed the scenarios match the M1
  success-criteria list from docs/ROADMAP.md

title: ft-cli work-graph commands
description:
The full M1 command surface for managing work.

**Crate:** ft-cli (extends E-M1-08)

**Commands:**
- Create: epic create, task create, subtask create, bug create
- Update: update <id> --status|--owner|--priority|--title
- Close: close <id> (validates ACs; --force --reason to override)
- Claim: claim <id>, unclaim <id> (mandatory claim_expires_at per ADR-0008)
- Link: link <from> <to> --type <kind>, dep add <from> <to> --type <kind>,
  dep remove <from> <to>
- Show: show <id> — full record with relations and history
- List: list [--type <t>] [--status <s>] [--owner <o>]
- Acceptance criteria: criteria add <id> "<text>", criteria list <id>,
  criteria check <id> <n>, criteria uncheck <id> <n>,
  criteria evidence <id> <n> --url <url>
- Ready: ready — list unblocked work
- Views: board, graph <id> (ASCII tree)

**Acceptance:**
- Every command has integration tests against TestRepo
- close refuses when ACs incomplete; --force --reason works
- claim is atomic: two concurrent claims on the same record — one wins, one fails
  with a clear error
- board and graph produce stable, snapshottable output (insta tests)
- --json output is documented and stable

title: ft-cli scaffold + init + doctor
description:
Clap entry point, command dispatch, and the two bootstrap commands.

**Crate:** ft-cli

**Commands shipped in this epic:**
- firetrail init — creates .firetrail/ structure, writes default config, installs
  git hooks (post-checkout, post-merge, pre-commit), writes .gitignore additions,
  optionally creates AGENTS.md / .claude/skills/firetrail/
- firetrail doctor — verifies config, storage, index, git status, schema version,
  reports issues with actionable fix hints

**Global options:**
- --format markdown|json (default: markdown for ttys, json for non-ttys)
- --quiet
- --verbose

**Acceptance:**
- firetrail init in an empty git repo produces a working .firetrail/ that
  subsequent commands can use without further setup
- firetrail doctor on a fresh init reports clean
- firetrail doctor after a manual corruption (delete index.db) reports the issue
  and suggests firetrail index rebuild
- All commands accept --json and produce machine-readable output
- Help text follows clap conventions; firetrail help <cmd> works

title: ft-identity basic resolution
description:
Implement basic identity resolution for M1. Full registry features are M5 (ADR-0008).

**Crate:** ft-identity

**Public API:**
- Identity re-exported from ft-core
- resolve_identity() returns the current actor, checking in order:
  1. FIRETRAIL_AUTHOR env var
  2. .firetrail/config.yml identity.name
  3. git config user.email
- Strict-mode flag in config: if true, fail loudly when no identity resolvable
- IdentityError type

**M5 deferred:** identity registry, aliases, capabilities, kinds, on-behalf-of,
offboarding sweep. M1 ships the resolution path only.

**Acceptance:**
- Resolution from each source works and is tested
- Strict mode rejects unknown identities
- Resolution failure produces a clear error message naming the order checked
- Integration test: TestRepo with various env var states resolves correctly

title: ft-index SQLite schema and basic queries
description:
Build the SQLite-backed read index. Vector search (sqlite-vec) is M3.

**Crate:** ft-index

**Public API:**
- Index::open(path) — open or create the index database at .firetrail/index.db
- Index::rebuild_from(storage) — full rebuild from the storage layer
- Index::refresh_incremental(storage, changed_paths) — diff-driven update
- Queries:
  - list_by(filter) with filters for type, status, owner, scope (scope is M5; for
    M1 just plumb the field through)
  - ready() — records with no unresolved blockers, not closed, not claimed
  - dependency_walk(id, direction) — walk blocks/blocked-by graph
  - show(id) — full record with relations
- IndexError

**SQLite schema (M1 subset):**
- records(id, type, title, status, priority, owner, created_by, created_at, ...)
- labels(record_id, key, value)
- relations(from_id, to_id, kind, created_at, created_by)
- acceptance_criteria(id, record_id, text, status, evidence_url, checked_by, ...)
- evidence(id, record_id, evidence_type, url, description, created_at, created_by)

**Acceptance:**
- Rebuild from a fixture corpus produces identical query results to direct file reads
- Incremental refresh handles add/modify/delete deltas correctly
- Ready detection respects blocked-by relations
- Performance: list-1000-records under 100ms; ready under 200ms
- Property tests on dependency walk

title: ft-storage embedded mode JSON read/write
description:
Implement the storage layer for embedded mode (ADR-0006). External mode is M5.

**Crate:** ft-storage

**Public API:**
- Storage trait: read_record(id), write_record(record), list_records(filter),
  delete_record(id), read_record_at_ref(ref, id)
- EmbeddedStorage implementation — operates against
  .firetrail/records/<type>/<id>.json
- Atomic write: write to <id>.json.tmp, rename to <id>.json
- Path conventions consistent with ADR-0002 and ADR-0015 (lowercase IDs)
- StorageError type

**Acceptance:**
- Round-trip: write a record, read it back, fields identical
- Concurrent writes from two threads do not corrupt records (atomic rename)
- List records by type returns expected set; pagination works
- Reading a record at a non-existent ref returns NotFound, not garbage
- Property tests for path encoding (handles unicode, special chars)
- Integration tests use TestRepo

title: ft-git git operations wrapper
description:
Wrap git operations behind a clean Rust API used by ft-storage and ft-cli.

**Crate:** ft-git

**Public API:**
- Repo::open(path), Repo::status(), Repo::head(), Repo::current_branch()
- Repo::list_files(ref, glob) — list files matching glob at a given ref
- Repo::read_file_at_ref(ref, path) — read file contents at a ref
- Repo::diff(from_ref, to_ref, path_filter)
- Repo::is_clean(), Repo::has_uncommitted(path)
- Repo::branches(), Repo::branch_create(name, from), Repo::branch_delete(name)
- Repo::install_hook(name, content) — install a post-checkout/post-merge hook
- Error types: GitError (thiserror)

**Implementation:** prefer gix for reads (modern, pure-Rust). Shell out to git
for operations that gix does not yet cover (e.g., complex merge operations later).

**Acceptance:**
- TestRepo from ft-testkit uses ft-git under the hood
- Read a file from main branch while checked out on a feature branch works
- All public methods have doc tests
- Property tests for path-glob filtering

title: ft-core record types and schema
description:
Implement the canonical record types for M1: Task, Subtask, Bug, Epic,
AcceptanceCriterion, Evidence. Plus shared fields and types.

**Crate:** ft-core

**Public API:**
- Record enum: Record::Task(Task), Record::Epic(Epic), etc.
- Per-type structs with strongly-typed fields (status, priority, owner are enums)
- RecordId newtype wrapper around String with hash-prefix display logic (ADR-0015)
- Identity newtype (ADR-0008, basic form)
- state_hash and prev_state_hash fields (computation logic is ft-history; ft-core
  only declares them)
- Acceptance criterion type with status, evidence_url, checked_by, checked_at
- Evidence type with evidence_type enum
- Serde implementations for canonical JSON serialization
- JSON Schema validation via jsonschema or hand-rolled validator
- Dependency relation enum (blocks, blocked-by, parent-of, etc. from FR-020)

**Acceptance:**
- All record types round-trip through serde_json
- Property tests (proptest) for record roundtrip on arbitrary inputs
- Schema validation rejects malformed records
- Hash computation is deterministic and excludes state_hash and prev_state_hash
  from its own input
- Display of RecordId produces unambiguous prefixes given a candidate set

title: ft-testkit foundation
description:
Build the test infrastructure that all other M1 epics consume.

**Crate:** ft-testkit

**Public API:**
- TestRepo::new() returns an isolated tempdir + initialized git repo + initialized
  .firetrail/ directory
- TestRepo::commit(msg), TestRepo::branch(name), TestRepo::checkout(name)
- Record factories: make_task(), make_epic(), make_finding(), etc. with builder-style
  overrides
- Assertion helpers: assert_record_exists(id), assert_field(id, field, value),
  assert_dep(from, to, kind)
- Scenario runner skeleton — parses YAML/RON scenario files, executes commands
  against a TestRepo, asserts expected state

**Acceptance:**
- A trivial scenario file ("create a task, assert it exists") runs and passes
- TestRepo cleanup is automatic (Drop impl removes tempdir)
- Factories produce records that round-trip cleanly through serde_json
- ft-testkit is a dependency of ft-core's test target (sanity check)

**Note:** Scenario runner only needs the skeleton at this stage. Full scenario
coverage lives in E-M1-10. Other M1 epics use TestRepo and factories.

title: Workspace skeleton + tooling
description:
Set up the Cargo workspace, crate scaffolding, CI workflow, and developer tooling.

**Crates created (empty skeletons):**
ft-core, ft-storage, ft-index, ft-embed, ft-identity, ft-scope, ft-trust,
ft-history, ft-search, ft-prime, ft-pr, ft-import, ft-git, ft-cli, ft-testkit.

**Deliverables:**
- Workspace `Cargo.toml` with shared dependencies declared via `[workspace.dependencies]`
- Each crate has its own `Cargo.toml` with placeholder lib.rs / main.rs
- `rustfmt.toml`, `.clippy.toml`, `rust-toolchain.toml` (stable, 2024 edition)
- `scripts/validate.sh` running fmt + clippy + nextest
- Pre-commit hook installer (writes to `.git/hooks/pre-commit`)
- GitHub Actions workflow running the full validation gate
- `justfile` (or `Makefile`) with common commands

**Acceptance:**
- `cargo build --workspace` succeeds with empty crates
- `cargo nextest run --workspace` runs (zero tests is OK at this stage)
- `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- `./scripts/validate.sh` exits 0
- CI workflow file exists and is syntactically valid

title: M1 — Local Work Graph
description:
Milestone: ship a usable local work-graph CLI.

A single engineer can run `firetrail init`, create epics and tasks, declare
dependencies, add acceptance criteria, claim work, and close work. Board view
shows state. Graph view shows blockers. JSON-in-Git storage with rebuildable
SQLite read index. Embedded mode only.

See docs/ROADMAP.md §M1 for the full scope and gates.

**Shippable promise.** Replace lightweight personal task tracking on a single repo.
Not yet competitive with full team tools (no memory, no search, no PR enforcement).

**Gates.**
- 100% of M1 acceptance criteria covered by tests
- Verifier subagent has signed off on each component PR
- `firetrail doctor` reports clean repo after the scripted scenario
- Single-binary distribution for macOS (arm64, x86_64) and Linux (x86_64)

  → tasks claimed and closed in dependency order → board reflects state at each step

  succeeds after ACs checked, force-close with reason works
- `tests/scenarios/m1-conflict-resolution.scenario` — two branches add tasks under
  the same epic; merge via JSON merge driver succeeds; both tasks present on main
- `tests/scenarios/m1-index-rebuild.scenario` — delete index.db; next command rebuilds
  cleanly; queries return same results as before deletion
- `tests/scenarios/m1-clean-clone.scenario` — clone the repo; first firetrail command
  works without manual setup

**Acceptance:**
- All M1 scenarios pass
- Total scenario suite runs in under 30 seconds (per ROADMAP §M1 success criteria)
- Each scenario has clear failure messages when assertions fail

title: Upgrade gix to drop RUSTSEC-2025-0140 / RUSTSEC-2025-0021 advisories
description: deny.toml ignores two transitive gix advisories (gix-path Non-utf8 String, sha1-collisiondetection). Revisit when gix upstream ships fixes and before PR import / untrusted commit ingestion lands (E-M1-10).

title: ft-index relations from explicit graph (currently inline-only)
description: ft-index derives parent-of / child-of edges from inline body fields (Task.parent_epic, Subtask.parent_task, Epic.child_ids). blocks / blocked-by relations are NOT writable from inline fields — tests inject directly into the SQL relations table. When ft-storage exposes a Relation graph, extend rebuild_from to consume it and drop the test-only side-channel.

title: Test
description: test

title: ft-identity: parse full ADR-0008 identities: registry block
description: .firetrail/identity.yml parses only name and email at M1; the full identities: registry block is silently ignored (forward-compatible). M5 should extend the deserializer.

title: ft-identity: wire StatusReport via ft-git (currently shells out)
description: ft-identity shells out to git config --get user.email/name directly to avoid coupling with the in-flux ft-git crate. Now that ft-git is stable, refactor to consume ft_git::StatusReport or equivalent. Touch point: crates/ft-identity/src/lib.rs git_config source.

title: ft-index: populate last_indexed_commit from ft-git::head()
description: Schema column exists; only last_rebuild_at is populated today. Wire ft_git::head() once stable so incremental refresh can short-circuit on no-op commits.

title: Replace ft-index local Storage shim with ft_storage::Storage
description: ft-index defines a local Storage trait + StorageError + StorageFilter in crates/ft-index/src/storage.rs as an interim because ft-storage is a stub. When ft-storage lands its canonical Storage trait, delete the shim and re-export from there. Also update IndexError::Storage(#[from] StorageError) to wrap the real type.

title: Harmonize ft-testkit record/relation paths with ft-storage
description: ft-testkit defines RECORDS_DIR + record_path() inline and stores relations as flat .firetrail/relations.json — interim until ft-storage owns the layout. When ft-storage lands, replace inline writes with delegation. Search crates/ft-testkit/src/paths.rs and assertions.rs for call sites.

title: Replace ft-testkit testkit: virtual commands with real firetrail binary
description: ft-testkit scenario runner has a testkit:create-task / testkit:create-epic / testkit:assert-exists dispatch as a stand-in because ft-cli is a stub. Once firetrail init/task create/etc. exist, port scenarios to call the real binary and remove (or feature-gate) the testkit: shortcuts. Touch points: crates/ft-testkit/src/scenario.rs and tests/scenarios/.

title: ft-testkit cross-crate sanity check from ft-core tests
description: ft-testkit spec acceptance #7 says ft-testkit must be consumed by ft-core's integration tests as a sanity check. Currently scoped only to ft-testkit's own tests. Add ft-testkit as a dev-dep to ft-core and write a test that uses TestRepo to round-trip a ft-core Record.

title: Prefix-based RecordId resolution from candidate set
description: RecordId::from_string requires full 64-char hex tail. CLI/storage layers need prefix-based resolution (ADR-0015) — accept a short prefix, return the unique RecordId from a candidate set or error on ambiguity. ft-core has unambiguous_display building block.

title: Export ft-core JSON schema to docs/schema/
description: ft-core::record_schema_json() exposes the schema in-memory. Wire a small script or ft-cli subcommand to write it to docs/schema/firetrail-record-v1.json so external consumers can pin against it. Mentioned as a follow-up in ft-core (firetrail-kss) acceptance review.

title: ft-storage: partial-parse optimization for list()
description: EmbeddedStorage::list / iter full-parses + schema-validates every file. Production uses ft-index so this is fine, but a partial-parse path (envelope only for filter, full only for matching records) helps large repos when ft-index is unavailable.

title: ft-storage: crash-injection atomicity test
description: Spec acceptance #2 calls for atomicity verification via fault injection (panic between sync_all and rename; assert no half-written file remains). Current parallel-race tests give equivalent confidence but not the spec-mandated form. Best after ft-cli lands so we can fork-and-kill a real binary.

title: ft-identity: implement strict-mode rejection via M5 registry
description: Strict mode is stored on the resolver and surfaced in ResolutionTrace, but doesn't reject. Spec acceptance #7 expects M5 to wire this to the registry. File for M5.

title: ft-index: performance gate behind --features slow-tests
description: Spec calls for a 1,000-record fixture perf test. Deferred at M1. Add behind feature flag so CI doesn't pay the cost by default.

title: Wire install_hooks config in ft-testkit TestRepoConfig
description: TestRepoConfig.install_hooks is currently a no-op. Wire it once ft-cli init --hooks (or equivalent) exists so scenarios can exercise hook behavior.

title: Nightly stress test for RecordId::mint uniqueness at scale
description: ADR-0015 acceptance implies 16M+ unique IDs should not collide. ft-core unit/proptest coverage caps at ~10K / 500 cases for CI speed. Add a #[ignore]'d test runnable in nightly CI to validate the literal acceptance bullet.

value: ft-identity (firetrail-c4c) shells out directly to git for user.email/user.name resolution; does NOT depend on ft-git. When ft-git's StatusReport API lands, ft-identity could be refactored to consume it (spec line 124 mentions this). Tracked as future M2/M5 polish, not required for M1.

ft-git (firetrail-vth) implementation: uses gix 0.66 with revision+blob-diff features for reads (head/current_branch/is_detached/branches/branch_exists/read_file_at_ref/list_files_at_ref/log_path/diff); shells out to git for status/is_clean/has_uncommitted/branch_create/branch_delete/checkout; direct fs for install_hook/hook_installed/remove_hook with managed-region markers. Adds globset 0.4 as direct dep (not workspace). Repo struct only stores root PathBuf — opens fresh gix::Repository per call to avoid lifetime leakage. log_path uses parent-blob comparison for correctness (not adjacent-walk diff).
